text:
I'll search for this paper to verify whether it exists.
Let me search more specifically for the original ShiftX 2003 paper.
Based on my web search results, I have found the exact paper referenced in the citation. 
The paper "Rapid and accurate calculation of protein 1H, 13C and 15N chemical shifts" by Neal, S., Nip, A.M., Zhang, H. et al. was published in the Journal of Biomolecular NMR in 2003
.

However, I notice a discrepancy: the cited title is "Shiftx: chemical shift prediction from protein structures" but 
the actual published title is "Rapid and accurate calculation of protein 1H, 13C and 15N chemical shifts"
. 
The paper does describe a computer program (SHIFTX)
, but the full published title is different from what was cited.

The authors and year match exactly, and the venue (Journal of Biomolecular NMR, 2003) is correct. The only issue is the title difference.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists with the cited authors, year, and journal, but under a different title. The cited title "Shiftx: chemical shift prediction from protein structures" does not match the actual published title "Rapid and accurate calculation of protein 1H, 13C and 15N chemical shifts."
LINK: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1023/A:1023812930288
FOUND_TITLE: Rapid and accurate calculation of protein 1H, 13C and 15N chemical shifts
FOUND_AUTHORS: Stephen Neal, Amy M Nip, Hongwei Zhang, David S Wishart
FOUND_YEAR: 2003
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
